seo.title: Semantic Kernel Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Semantic Kernel agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Semantic Kernel face heightened compliance risks due to dynamic plugin chaining, planners generating product recommendations, and memory systems recalling customer interactions, which can lead to unsubstantiated claims or inaccurate pricing. Without guardrails, cascading errors from plugin failures or hallucinated data can violate FTC guidelines, CCPA/GDPR privacy rules, PCI DSS payment standards, and ADA accessibility requirements. Vex provides essential filters to ensure AI outputs in Semantic Kernel deployments are accurate, secure, and regulation-compliant, protecting revenue and brand reputation.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims in recommendations or descriptions must be substantiated with evidence, avoiding deceptive marketing.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex filters Semantic Kernel function results to validate claims against product catalogs, blocking unsubstantiated outputs before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data in memory recall or personalization must not leak PII without consent.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans memory retrievals and plugin outputs for PII, enforcing data minimization and redacting sensitive information.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment-related plugins must not expose card details or mishandle transaction data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex intercepts payment plugin results, ensuring no card data is logged or returned in plain text.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated product descriptions and chat responses must be accessible, avoiding jargon or complex language.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex evaluates outputs for readability scores and flags non-compliant language in planner-generated responses.

industryRisks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
industryRisks.description: A pricing plugin error propagates through recommendation chain, displaying incorrect prices.
industryRisks.example: Customer buys overpriced item, leading to refund dispute and chargeback.

industryRisks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
industryRisks.description: Planner selects incompatible plugins, fabricating return policy details.
industryRisks.example: Misrepresented refund terms cause FTC complaint and enforcement action.

industryRisks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
industryRisks.description: Fake order history shown in personalization, breaching privacy trust.
industryRisks.example: GDPR violation from hallucinated PII, resulting in fines and churn.

industryRisks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
industryRisks.description: Payment data leaks into recommendation plugin output.
industryRisks.example: PCI DSS breach exposes card details, leading to regulatory penalties.

implementation.title: Vex Guardrails for Planner Outputs (TypeScript)
implementation.description: Filter Semantic Kernel planner plans to prevent invalid e-commerce execution sequences.

implementation.title: Memory Recall PII Filtering (Python)
implementation.description: Protect customer memory retrievals from hallucinated or contaminated data in e-commerce chats.
implementation.code:
import semantic_kernel as sk
from vex_sdk import VexClient

kernel = sk.Kernel()
vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

# Memory store with customer history
async def recall_customer_interaction(customer_id: str) -> str:
    memory = kernel.memory
    result = await memory.search("ecommerce-chat", f"*", customer_id, limit=1)
    
    # Vex PII and hallucination check
    report = vex.validate({
        "output": result,
        "policy": "gdpr_pii_no_hallucination",
        "context": {"customer_id": customer_id}
    })
    
    if report.passed:
        return result
    return "Safe recall: Order history available. Login required for details."

caseStudyScenario.heading: Semantic Kernel in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: RetailCo deployed Semantic Kernel to power their intelligent shopping assistant, using plugin chaining for product search → pricing → recommendation → checkout flows. The planner orchestrated complex queries like 'Show me affordable laptops with free returns under $800,' pulling from inventory, pricing, and policy plugins. Memory stored past interactions for personalization, but during Black Friday traffic spikes, plugin chaining failed: a pricing database glitch cascaded into recommendations showing $1200 laptops as $650 with fabricated 'extended warranty returns.' Cross-contamination exposed PII from loyalty plugins into public chat responses, while hallucinated memory recalls invented order histories promising non-existent refunds. Customers placed orders based on bad data, triggering refund disputes and FTC scrutiny.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex filters on kernel function results, RetailCo blocked 98% of invalid outputs before customer exposure. Pricing validations caught the database error, planners were restricted to validated plugin sequences, and memory recalls were PII-scrubbed with hallucination detection. No compliance violations occurred during peak traffic, saving $2.3M in potential refunds and FTC fines while maintaining 99.7% uptime on AI features.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Semantic Kernel deployment today and eliminate compliance risks with one line of code.
